text:
Market today: The mood for tech, especially chips, is turning cautious. Recent headlines point to chip stocks losing their luster and selling off after Samsung's earnings missed AI expectations. This sentiment is the real driver, directly impacting your concentrated book.

Your holdings: SOXL is a major concern, down -35.06% and a leveraged ETF. SPAL, another new leveraged ETF, is also down -23.1%. Both amplify drops on margin. QQQI needs attention if its 52 stop is hit.

Watch: Keep a very close eye on your leveraged chip exposure (SOXL, SMH) given the negative chip news. Also, monitor the 52 stop on QQQI.

Bottom line: Prioritize risk management on your leveraged and underperforming chip positions.
plays: Act on SOXL: Consider trimming this leveraged position given its -35.06% loss and chip sector weakness. Respect the 200 stop.
Monitor QQQI: Be ready to trim QQQI if it breaks its 52 stop, as planned for this income anchor.
Manage SPAL: This new, leveraged position is down -23.1%. Ensure you cut if it hits its 21.38 stop.
